title: Feature workflow state
description: Schema for .claude/features/<TICKET>/state.json (or .claude/features/_pr-<NUMBER>/state.json in PR-only review mode), the durable progress record for a single feature workflow run.

ticket.description: Tracker ticket ID, e.g. ABC-1234. Pattern is configurable per project in .claude/commands/feature.md. Null in PR-only review mode (workspace .claude/features/_pr-<NUMBER>/), where review runs without a feature workspace.

branch.description: Git branch created in Stage 3. Null before implementation begins.

stage.description: Current stage. Workflow resumes from here on re-invocation.

owns_branch.description: True if the head branch lives in this repo (not a fork) and we can push to it. Written explicitly by the conductor in both modes — Stage 4 (open PR) sets it true after creating the branch in ticket mode; the PR-only review entry computes it from the ownership check (same-repo head AND `gh pr checkout` success). Stage 5's auto-fix loop gates on a strict `=== true` check, so this field MUST be present whenever the loop runs.

exit_reason.description: How the loop exited. 'clean': will_fix empty, PR ready to merge. 'unpushable': head branch is on a fork or otherwise un-pushable; will-fix surfaced as a punch list. 'max_rounds_exhausted': hit max_rounds with unresolved will-fix; in modes where we own the branch the user can still choose to iterate. Null while the loop is in progress.

asset.description: Path to the stage's output artifact (brief.md, tasks.md, etc.).

thread_id.description: GraphQL review-thread node ID. Required so the fix subagent can call resolveReviewThread after applying the fix.
